messages.content:
Match each type of migration or dispersal to what follows it. A snail, salmon, dandelion, and butterflies.

passive dispersal
daily movement
one direction only
one-return journey

Snail - Daily movement
Salmon - One-return journey
Dandelion - Passive dispersal
Butterflies - One direction only

Two grizzly bears are fighting over a fish to feed themselves. What kind of interaction is this scenario, which works as a limiting factor in the bears' survival?

 A. 
interference competition
 B. 
exploitation competition
 C. 
mutualism
 D. 
apparent competition

What refers to the sequence of events in an organism's life?

A 
 refers to the sequence of events in an organism's life from birth to 
.

Which two sentences describe survivorship curves?

 There are six types of survivorship curves.
 These curves divide an organism's population into age groups to show how long the members of each age group will live.
 They help to compare life histories of different populations.
 These curves show the number of individuals living at each age interval plotted versus time.
 They are also known as a mortality tables.

These curves divide an organism's population into age groups to show how long the members of each age group will live.

They help to compare life histories of different populations.

If the government decides to divide someone's private property into different geographic districts, which two laws and regulations would be involved?

 Zoning
 Eminent domain
 Trespassing
 Easement

Match the stages to their features.

low birth rate with death rate being
constant
high birth rate and low death rate
high birth and death rates
Preindustrial
arrowRight
Mature industrial
arrowRight
Industrialization
arrowRight

Low birth rate with death rate being constant - Mature industrial stage
High birth rate and low death rate - Preindustrial stage
High birth and death rates - Industrialization stage

Which rate indicates the number of children that would be born per woman if she were to live to the end of her childbearing years?

 A. 
mortality rate
 B. 
birth rate
 C. 
fertility rate
 D. 
death rate

To what extent will greenhouse gases currently in the atmosphere impact future generations?

 A. 
The amount will impact several generations to come.
 B. 
The amount will only impact the current generation.
 C. 
The amount will only impact the next generation.
 D. 
The amount has no impact on any generation.
A. The amount will impact several generations to come.

Match the consequence of land use to its impact.

Areas open recreational parks
to increase revenue (money).
Zoning laws are passed.
People work together for the
good of the community.
Deforestation destroys habitats.
social
arrowRight
political
arrowRight
economic
arrowRight
environmental
arrowRight

Areas open recreational parks to increase revenue (money). - Economic consequence

Zoning laws are passed. - Political consequence

People work together for the good of the community. - Social consequence

Deforestation destroys habitats. - Environmental consequence
